name: Iron Fan
description: The Iron Fan (鐵扇公主, Tiě Shàn Gōngzhǔ), also known as Princess Iron Fan or Rakshasi, is a magical fan wielded by Princess Iron Fan, a prominent demoness in the classic Chinese novel *Journey to the West*. This fan is not merely a tool for cooling oneself; it possesses immense power capable of creating powerful gusts of wind, summoning storms, and extinguishing the raging flames of the Flaming Mountains, a formidable obstacle on Xuanzang's pilgrimage to the West. The fan was originally a palm leaf from a giant banana tree growing in the Kunlun Mountains. Through centuries of absorbing spiritual energy, it transformed into a magical artifact. It is said that the fan can only be used correctly with a specific incantation, and each wave has a different effect: the first wave extinguishes the flames, the second creates a cooling breeze, and the third summons a torrential downpour. Obtaining the fan is crucial for Sun Wukong to continue his journey, leading to numerous encounters and battles with Princess Iron Fan, who is initially reluctant to lend it due to Sun Wukong's past conflict with her husband, the Bull Demon King. The Iron Fan symbolizes control over the elements and the power to overcome seemingly insurmountable obstacles, representing a vital instrument in Xuanzang's quest for enlightenment and the triumph over adversity.
subtitle: The magical fan that controls the elements.
origin: Originally a palm leaf from a giant banana tree in the Kunlun Mountains, imbued with spiritual energy.
currentLocation: Varies depending on the telling, often with Princess Iron Fan or hidden away.

keyMyths.title: The Flaming Mountains
keyMyths.description: The Flaming Mountains block the path to the West, and only the Iron Fan can extinguish the flames. Sun Wukong must acquire the fan from Princess Iron Fan to continue the journey.

keyMyths.title: Conflict with Princess Iron Fan
keyMyths.description: Princess Iron Fan refuses to lend the fan due to Sun Wukong's past troubles with her husband, the Bull Demon King. This leads to battles and trickery as Sun Wukong tries to obtain the fan.

sources.author: Wu Cheng'en
sources.description: The primary source for the Iron Fan and Princess Iron Fan's story.
